originalTitle: Spain beat Argentina 1-0 after extra time to win the 2026 World Cup – as it happened
summary: Ferran Torres’ extra-time goal gave Spain a deserved victory over a negative Argentina in New Jersey
content: It’s time to wrap up our live coverage of the final, and of the 2026 World Cup. On behalf of all the liveloggers and editors, thanks for your company and emails throughout the tournament. I’ll leave you with David Hytner’s report of a deserved victory for Spain in the final. Goodnight! It ended almost as Gianni Infantino and Donald Trump had planned. Earlier on a seemingly endless World Cup final day the trophy had, in a scene perfectly of a piece with the event’s excess, been let out of a Louis Vuitton trunk. Now they shuffled up to the podium together and presented it to a beaming Rodri, fumbling the moment slightly when Trump lingered for Spain’s team photo. Within moments they had made their exit, golden glitter sparkling on a bobbly pitch and the reverberations from a football event of unprecedented scale pulsing far beyond this concreted mini-state in New Jersey. How to make sense of a heady, intoxicating, often deeply strange tournament that at once appeared fulsomely welcoming and chillingly distant? It concluded with a disorientating fever dream beneath a bright blue American sky, football appearing a sideshow for much of the afternoon even if the game did not exactly help itself. Anyone straining for a parable could, if they wanted, take pleasure in Spain’s success. This was ultimately the triumph of a collective idea in a World Cup nurtured to indulge the cult of the individual. More from Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni I feel sad. One could say many things about how we got here, but it’s not worth it. Endless thanks to these players who once again took us to the final. If they give everything the way they did today, it sets a great example for ‌the people and for our country. You have to pick yourself up again. When you leave everything like that, it’s very hard to find fault with anything.” [On Lionel Messi] He’s 39 years old now. It was crystal clear for me that he was going to play up until he decided not to. I hope that everyone feels proud of him, of what he’s achieved because he’s the best football player ever to set foot on a pitch. I have no doubt about this, and what he did during this World Cup was incredible. But also what he did before this. [On his own future] I will talk to the president. I believe it’s only ‌fair that I can take this time for myself to think this through. I never thought I would get to this place and it’s wonderful. In order to continue, you need a great deal of things, resetting your mind, rebooting, creating a group like this again – that is very difficult. I am very sorry. Neither the approach of yet another enervating heatwave, nor the prospect of heading to work on Monday morning, was going to keep Madrileños huddled indoors when the World Cup, and the stifling streets of the capital, were there for the taking. With the temperature at 9pm nudging 32C, people across the city and the wider region filled bars, parks, fairgrounds and bullrings to drink, to stripe the streets red-and-yellow with their rojigualda flags, and to test their lungs, their nerves, and, as it turned out, their boredom thresholds. In the capital itself, the faithful descended on a giant screen by the river Manzanares and trooped into the cavernous concrete confines of the 15,000-capacity Movistar arena. Most, however, appeared to have made for the Plaza de Colón to watch the game under the statue of Christopher Columbus, for whom the square is named. Overhead, sharp against a bright blue July sky, flew the largest flag in Spain: a vexillological behemoth that measures 21m by 14m and weighs 35kg. He wasn’t wrong, was he? Charles De Ketelaere is the answer to a future quiz question. He was the only man to score against Spain at this World Cup in 12 hours of football. They never went behind, they were only taken to extra-time in the final. In the parlance of our times, they’re a beautiful, brilliant football team. Luis de la Fuente is 65 years old and has never won a major club competition as a manager. He is also one of the top 20 managers of all time. Lionel Scaloni’s reaction They (Spain) were better, that’s the truth. But I’ll hold on to an enormous memory of what they’ve done, of what it’s worth to get this far. We’re great in victory and we have to be great in defeat. Today we’re showing that we know how to lose. We lost the match and we’re owning it. But that doesn’t mean we’ll stop remembering everything we did to get here. Rodri, a giant of a human being and a pretty good footballer too, reflects on Spain’s triumph and his comeback from injury I cannot express it with words. It’s like we’re in a cloud. It’s been a very tough time. I just want the new generations to see my example as an opportunity – when you go down you can rise again. This is my philosophy my entire life. Of course, sometimes things go well, things they go wrong. But always the positivity I said to the lads before the game – we have to go for the game. We need to face them. We have to look to the eyes and see it. We won this game and then life gives you the World Cup or not. But we were brave and I was brave in that moment. Thanks to Francis Fowles for this cracking stat (assuming it’s correct) Only four players have won the World Cup, European Championship, European Cup/Champions League and Ballon d’Or: Franz Beckenbauer Gerd Muller Zinedine Zidane Rodri Ed Aarons’ player ratings Pau Cubarsí Got away with his early error and didn’t put a foot wrong after that. Incredible that he is still only 19. 8 Mikel Merino’s verdict Unbelievable. It’s the perfect moment for us – we’re such a golden generation with these young players who give everything for this country, for these fans. It was a game about concentration. We knew we were a better team in terms of football. The pitch was dry and they know how to make the game difficult to you. I think the team was outstanding today and, yeah, we deserved it. When you are facing a low block, you have to attack the box with bodies on the line and control the t
chineseTitle: 西班牙经过加时赛以 1-0 击败阿根廷，夺得 2026 年世界杯冠军——事实如此
chineseSummary: 费兰·托雷斯在加时赛中的进球让西班牙队在新泽西队战胜了负于对手的阿根廷队
chineseContent: 是时候结束我们对决赛和 2026 年世界杯的现场报道了。我代表所有现场记录者和编辑，感谢您在整个比赛期间的陪伴和电子邮件。我将给您留下大卫·海特纳 (David Hytner) 的报道，西班牙队在决赛中获得了当之无愧的胜利。晚安！事情几乎按照詹尼·因凡蒂诺和唐纳德·特朗普的计划结束了。早些时候，在看似无休无止的世界杯最后一天，奖杯从路易威登的行李箱中取出，与赛事的奢华程度完美契合。现在，他们一起拖着脚步走上讲台，把它交给了笑容满面的罗德里，当特朗普在西班牙队合影时，他们稍微摸索了一下。不一会儿，他们就离开了，金色的光芒在崎岖不平的球场上闪闪发光，一场规模空前的足球赛事的回响远远超出了新泽西州这个混凝土小州的范围。如何理解一场令人陶醉、令人陶醉、常常非常奇怪、既显得热烈欢迎又显得冷漠疏远的锦标赛？比赛结束时，在美国明亮的蓝色天空下，一场令人迷失方向的狂热梦境结束了，整个下午的大部分时间里，足球似乎只是一场余兴节目，尽管比赛并没有完全发挥作用。任何苦苦思索寓言的人，如果愿意的话，都可以为西班牙的成功感到高兴。这最终是世界杯上为满足个人崇拜而培育的集体理念的胜利。更多来自阿根廷主帅莱昂内尔·斯卡罗尼的信息 我感到很难过。关于我们如何走到这一步，人们可以说很多话，但这不值得。衷心感谢这些再次带领我们进入决赛的球员。如果他们像今天一样付出一切，这将为人民和我们的国家树立一个很好的榜样。你必须重新振作起来。当你就这样离开一切时，那真是太棒了
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
